name: Behalf.bot — Restaurant booking (TheFork)
description: Book a restaurant table via TheFork (Lisbon-strong). Triggers on natural-language booking requests from Discord. Handles the full flow: parse intent via Claude Haiku, drive TheFork via Playwright, soft-confirm with the installer via Discord reaction, submit booking, optionally create a Google Calendar event. Built for installer-2's install as a productized feature; not actively used by Sean.

enabled.description: Master switch. Set true to activate the plugin in an installer's chassis. Off by default since the plugin requires installer-specific TheFork credentials in Vaultwarden.

time_zone.description: IANA timezone name for datetime parsing + display (e.g. 'Europe/Lisbon', 'America/New_York'). All booking times in the parse / confirm / create-cal pipeline use this zone.

default_lang.description: TheFork UI language preference when selecting time slots.

max_party_size.description: Party size threshold above which the soft-confirm message gets a 'large party' warning. Bookings above this still attempt; the warning lets the installer reject before submission.

discord_channel_id.description: Discord channel ID where the soft-confirm message is posted. Typically the installer's main working channel (e.g. #<primary>, #asimov-ops). Reaction polling watches this channel only.

confirm_timeout_seconds.description: Soft-confirm timeout in seconds. After this with no reaction, the booking is aborted (no submission to TheFork). V1 default is 600s (10 min) per the spec.

thefork_base_url.description: TheFork base URL. Use https://www.thefork.com for the default EN locale, https://www.thefork.pt for native PT, etc. The booking script appends locale-specific paths.

vaultwarden_item_name.description: Name of the Vaultwarden Login item holding TheFork credentials (username + password fields). The booking script pulls credentials at invocation time via the chassis bw-CLI hydration path; never stored in .env.

screenshot_dir.description: Directory under the plugin root where per-booking screenshots are saved (gitignored). 'logs' (default) keeps them with the booking audit. Override to '/tmp' for ephemeral storage that disappears on container restart.

time_slot_tolerance_minutes.description: Search window (±N minutes) around the requested booking time. TheFork rarely has exact-minute slots; 30 min default gives a reasonable spread without surfacing wildly-different alternatives.

gcal_calendar_id.description: Google Calendar ID where confirmed bookings get added as events. 'primary' uses the installer's default calendar. Set to a specific calendar ID for separation (e.g. a 'Personal' calendar shared with their household).

activation._note: Dormant by default. Activation gated on installer setting modules.restaurant-booking.enabled: true in chassis.config.yaml AND populating the Vaultwarden TheFork-credentials item. The plugin is invoked by the main session when an installer's Discord message matches a booking intent — there's no continuous heartbeat watching for these.

_chassis_v1_notes.trigger_invocation: Currently invoked via the main session's intent detection rather than a regex-matched chassis trigger. The skill at skills/restaurant-booking.md describes the conversational flow; the four scripts under scripts/ implement each pipeline stage. If/when a chassis-level trigger registry lands, this plugin can register a 'book a table for ...' pattern but for V1 the model-driven dispatch is sufficient.
_chassis_v1_notes.external_deps: scripts use the playwright npm package (declared in package.json) for the TheFork browser-automation flow. Installers must `npm install` inside plugins/restaurant-booking/ before first use OR the chassis Dockerfile must pre-install playwright + chromium at the image-baked layer (deferred — most installs won't enable this plugin).
_chassis_v1_notes.credentials: TheFork credentials live in Vaultwarden under modules.restaurant-booking.vaultwarden_item_name (default 'thefork-credentials'). Username + password fields; pulled at booking time via the chassis bw-CLI hydration path. NEVER stored in .env.
_chassis_v1_notes.soft_confirm_pattern: Per the skill, the plugin sends a Discord message describing the proposed booking with two buttons (✅/❌). Reaction polling waits up to confirm_timeout_seconds for the installer's response before submitting OR aborting. This is the 'soft-confirm' gate installer-2's install spec calls for — no auto-submission without explicit reaction.
_chassis_v1_notes.installer_target: Built for installer-2's install (per <v1-reference-install>#618 umbrella + memory `lead_marc_johnson`). Not actively used by Sean's install — restaurant booking isn't part of his daily flow. The OpenClaw port lands the wrapping so the plugin can ship as a discoverable chassis module once Marc's Docker install reaches the relevant phase.
